'''Francis Joseph Mihaka Watene''' (born 15 February 1977) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] ([[1995 Rugby League World Cup]] squad), New Zealand [[Junior Kiwis]] (1996), [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]], and at club level for [[Counties Manukau Heroes]], [[New Zealand Warriors|Auckland Warriors]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] at club level.<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Personal==
Frank is the older brother of [[rugby league]] footballer, [[Adam Watene]].

==Early years==
Watene started his career with the [[Otahuhu Leopards]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition, before being signed by the new [[Auckland Warriors]] club in 1994.<ref>Richard Becht. ''A New Breed Rising: The Warriors Winfield Cup Challenge''. Auckland, HarperCollins, 1994. {{ISBN|1-86950-154-3}}. p.184</ref> He made his first grade début in 1998. Watene toured with the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team]] in 1998 and played for them in the [[1997 Oceania Cup]] and against Great Britain in 1999.<ref name="Māori">''New Zealand Rugby League Annual '98'', [[New Zealand Rugby League]], 1998. p.118</ref><ref name="NZ_Herald_50093">{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=50093|title=Rugby League: It's a different world for Maori|author=Jessup, Peter|date=3 November 1999|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=30 September 2011}}</ref>

==England==
Watene last played for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the second division of [[England|English]] rugby league called the [[RFL Championship]].
Of Tongan and New Zealand [[Māori people|Māori]] descent, 'Frank the Tank' signed for Halifax at the start of the 2007 rugby league season after playing for [[Dewsbury Rams]] in 2006 and helping them to win their National League two promotion. Watene was named in the dream team after an impressive year. Watene's usual position is Prop Forward however due to his sheer size Watene usually starts off the matches on the substitutes [[Sidelines|bench]] and will come onto the field of play later in the match, when his team need a boost of [[energy]] which he provides in the form of strong [[running]].{{citation needed|date=May 2022}}
In September 2007 Frank signed a one-year extension to his current [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] contract, after a highly impressive season.{{citation needed|date=May 2022}}
